title: Any Info on End of Evangelion Behind the Scenes
display_title: Any Info on End of Evangelion Behind the Scenes
wikitext:
[]: Any Info on End of Evangelion Behind the Scenes?             Quote Post by Archius » Thu Apr 09, 2015 10:28 pm Lately I've really been curious about the production of EoE, and I've heard stories about things like the voice recording sessions, or various things going on with live action segments, but I'd really like to hear some firsthand or official accounts of things. If there is such a document, is there anywhere I could read it? Any info would be appreciated, thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Apr 17, 2015 6:49 pm Bump. I'm kinda curious about this, too. Is there any first hand accounts of the behind the scenes of EoE? Even links to topics that have already discussed it would be fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Apr 17, 2015 9:58 pm There are only a couple tidbits of info that I know about this topic. -The first is the generally well-known anecdote about how Anno, Ogata, and Miyamura couldn't figure out how the originally scripted final line by Asuka ("I'd hate to be killed by someone like you!"....or something similar) should be delivered. Anno suggested a bunch of things including Ogata physically strangling Miyamura in order to get a proper emotional response, but none of them were satisfied. Finally, Anno had an idea and asked Miyamura to imagine that a stranger was masturbating over her and she couldn't do anything about it. He asked her how she would feel in such a situation and she responded "Disgusting." Anno replied with something along the lines of "I thought as much," and that's how the final line came to be. -The second is the deleted opening scene , which made it as far as the storyboard stage. In it, Toji and Kensuke are at the school basketball court while Shinji mopes in the background. Toji is in a wheelchair and shoots baskets while Kensuke films things with his camera. A conversation takes place about how they're all being transported away soon to Germany. Shinji remains silent throughout. Toji thanks Shinji for everything and him and Kensuke bid farewell to Shinji and that's basically the scene. It was probably cut because having Toji and/or Kensuke here would completely negate both how isolated Shinji is supposed to be at this point in the story as well as the bleak tone that sets the stage for the rest of the film.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Apr 18, 2015 6:20 am I remember reading about those two many months back, but does anyone else have any more info if there is some? Although I'm guessing they kept everything under wraps and behind closed doors, or at least for the most part.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Thu Apr 23, 2015 5:29 am Guy Nacks wrote: -The second is the deleted opening scene , which made it as far as the storyboard stage. In it, Toji and Kensuke are at the school basketball court while Shinji mopes in the background. Toji is in a wheelchair and shoots baskets while Kensuke films things with his camera. A conversation takes place about how they're all being transported away soon to Germany. Shinji remains silent throughout. Toji thanks Shinji for everything and him and Kensuke bid farewell to Shinji and that's basically the scene. It was probably cut because having Toji and/or Kensuke here would completely negate both how isolated Shinji is supposed to be at this point in the story as well as the bleak tone that sets the stage for the rest of the film.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Apr 23, 2015 12:34 pm Don't forget about the live-action mini-movie that was cut out of the 'end of your dream' bit. Toji and Asuka shacking up, who knew?

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Apr 23, 2015 6:43 pm NemZ wrote: Don't forget about the live-action mini-movie that was cut out of the 'end of your dream' bit. Toji and Asuka shacking up, who knew?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Apr 23, 2015 8:03 pm xPearse wrote: Interesting. Anymore info on that or is that it?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Apr 24, 2015 12:59 am robersora wrote: You can even watch it, if you look for it

[]:             Quote Post by shinhito » Sun Apr 26, 2015 5:08 am I don't have anything magnificent to add really except to say that I am too interested in more behind the scenes stuff for EoE and Evangelion in general. The Renewal DVDs have some interesting things that were sadly left out of the Platinum releases. Also, one of my favorite articles is about some items that were changed or left out from the End of Evangelion screenplays. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:End_of_Evangelion_Screenplays

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Apr 26, 2015 6:45 am Mr. Tines wrote: Like here : http://www.evamonkey.com/alternate-live-action-sequence/

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Apr 26, 2015 7:08 am shinhito wrote: I don't have anything magnificent to add really except to say that I am too interested in more behind the scenes stuff for EoE and Evangelion in general. The Renewal DVDs have some interesting things that were sadly left out of the Platinum releases. Also, one of my favorite articles is about some items that were changed or left out from the End of Evangelion screenplays. http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:End_of_Evangelion_Screenplays

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 26, 2015 7:14 am jcmoorehead wrote: I did always wonder if maybe there was a more 'positive ending' planned at one point or something else entirely.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Apr 26, 2015 7:16 am Mr. Tines wrote: I guess you could call Last A slightly more positive.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Apr 26, 2015 7:18 am xPearse wrote: Was it anything major [left out]? Or was it just a few misc things?

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Apr 26, 2015 7:21 pm pwhodges wrote: The films and stuff related to them; because Platinum is only the series as the films were licensed to a different company in the US/UK.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Apr 27, 2015 12:48 pm Guy Nacks wrote: It was probably cut because having Toji and/or Kensuke here would completely negate both how isolated Shinji is supposed to be at this point in the story as well as the bleak tone that sets the stage for the rest of the film.
